title: Phanera pyrrhoclada
intro: Phanera pyrrhoclada, also known as the Indian birinch, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly found in tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, skin infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Phanera pyrrhoclada include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phanera pyrrhoclada is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as in the form of a powder or extract, to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Phanera pyrrhoclada is classified under the family Apocynaceae and the genus Phanera. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a type of climbing plant. The species is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in countries such as India and China. Phanera pyrrhoclada is further classified under the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plants with similar characteristics. Its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
